feedback.text: Good Day, The Digital Fairness Act should include video games and consumers within the EU should guarantee that video games remain playable even after the active support of developers and publishers, as well as in case of a non-existent internet connection. I am giving these thoughts as Swiss, as I believe that this legislature can position the EU as a pioneer for digital customer rights and consumer protection, which will lead to similar legislatures in surrounding non-EU countries. Private companies should not retain full power over the legitimate ownership of consumers, especially in the field of art, where video games definitely have their place. Many thanks
